ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 ROKRAT C2 via legitimate cloud-storage APIs (pCloud / Yandex / Dropbox) from non-browser processes
ucs.description: Hunts ScarCruft/Reaper's flagship ROKRAT (and CHINOTTO) C2 channel — abuse of legitimate cloud-storage REST APIs (api.pcloud.com, cloud-api.yandex.net, *.dropboxapi.com) as bi-directional C2. The malware fetches OAuth-bearer commands and exfils to these endpoints. Surfaces hits only when the process is NOT a known browser or vendor sync client — exactly the shape ROKRAT presents.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as dest_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest IN ("api.pcloud.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com") NOT All_Traffic.app IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","Dropbox.exe","YandexDisk.exe","pcloud.exe","OneDrive.exe","slack.exe","Teams.exe") NOT All_Traffic.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | rename app as process_name, dest as remote_host | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// APT37 / ScarCruft — ROKRAT cloud-API C2 from non-browser processes
let _cloud_c2_hosts = dynamic(["api.pcloud.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","oauth2.pcloud.com"]);
let _benign_clients = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","iexplore.exe","safari.exe","Dropbox.exe","YandexDisk.exe","pcloud.exe","OneDrive.exe","OneDriveStandaloneUpdater.exe","msteams.exe","Teams.exe","slack.exe","Outlook.exe"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (_cloud_c2_hosts)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_benign_clients)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| where not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files\")
   or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\")
| summarize HitCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            Urls      = make_set(RemoteUrl, 25),
            Ports     = make_set(RemotePort, 10),
            SampleCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
            by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName,
               InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessSHA256
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT37 / ScarCruft — ROKRAT cloud-API C2 (ASIM port)
let _cloud_c2_hosts = dynamic(["api.pcloud.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","oauth2.pcloud.com"]);
let _benign_clients = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","iexplore.exe","Dropbox.exe","YandexDisk.exe","pcloud.exe","OneDrive.exe","msteams.exe","Teams.exe","slack.exe","Outlook.exe"]);
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where (DstHostname has_any (_cloud_c2_hosts)) or (Url has_any (_cloud_c2_hosts))
| where ActingProcessName !in~ (_benign_clients)
| where SrcUsername !endswith "$"
| summarize HitCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            Hosts     = make_set(DstHostname, 25),
            Ports     = make_set(DstPortNumber, 10),
            SampleCmd = any(ActingProcessCommandLine)
            by DvcHostname, SrcUsername, ActingProcessName
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT37 ScarCruft ROKRAT C2 via Cloud Storage APIs
id: 7a9c4f12-b2e1-4d8b-9a13-rokrat-cloud-c2
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects ROKRAT/CHINOTTO command-and-control traffic from APT37 (ScarCruft) — non-browser, non-sync-client processes
  connecting to legitimate cloud-storage REST endpoints (pCloud, Yandex Cloud, Dropbox) used as bidirectional C2.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0067/
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/rokrat-analysis
  - https://www.genians.co.kr/en/blog/threat_intelligence_report_apt37
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/11
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.g0067
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_dest:
    DestinationHostname:
      - 'api.pcloud.com'
      - 'cloud-api.yandex.net'
      - 'content.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'api.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'oauth2.pcloud.com'
  exclusion_clients:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\brave.exe'
      - '\opera.exe'
      - '\iexplore.exe'
      - '\Dropbox.exe'
      - '\YandexDisk.exe'
      - '\pcloud.exe'
      - '\OneDrive.exe'
      - '\msteams.exe'
      - '\Teams.exe'
      - '\slack.exe'
      - '\Outlook.exe'
  condition: selection_dest and not exclusion_clients
fields:
  - Image
  - User
  - DestinationHostname
  - DestinationPort
falsepositives:
  - Custom-named or repackaged sync clients
  - Developer tooling integrating directly with the pCloud/Yandex/Dropbox API
level: high
ucs.rationale: Cloud-storage REST APIs as C2 are the single most distinctive ROKRAT/CHINOTTO TTP — documented across Cisco Talos, Genians, AhnLab, Kaspersky and Volexity write-ups since 2017 and still observed in 2024-26 campaigns. Other actors use Dropbox occasionally, but APT37 is the only crew that has institutionalised pCloud + Yandex Cloud + Dropbox simultaneously as their primary command channel. Combined

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 ScarCruft LNK lure payload extraction via findstr against parent LNK file
ucs.description: Hunts the signature ScarCruft weaponised-LNK execution chain: explorer.exe opens a spearphish LNK, which spawns cmd.exe/powershell.exe that calls findstr.exe against its own .lnk path to carve out an embedded payload + decoy HWP/PDF. This findstr-on-.lnk invocation is essentially never seen in benign Windows usage and has been the loader pattern across multiple 2022-26 ScarCruft campaigns (RoKRAT, CHINOTTO, M2RAT).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as commandline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_commandline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=findstr.exe OR Processes.process_name=find.exe) Processes.parent_process_name IN (cmd.exe,powershell.exe,pwsh.exe) Processes.process="*.lnk*" NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// APT37 / ScarCruft — findstr against a .lnk file = ROKRAT/M2RAT/CHINOTTO loader carving its own payload
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("findstr.exe","find.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has ".lnk"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
// Optional pivot — co-occurring decoy/payload drop near the same DeviceId within 60s
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\")
    | where FileName endswith ".hwp" or FileName endswith ".pdf"
       or FileName endswith ".dat" or FileName endswith ".exe"
       or FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".bat"
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DroppedFile = FileName, DroppedPath = FolderPath
  ) on DeviceId
| where isnull(DropTime) or DropTime between (Timestamp - 60s .. Timestamp + 60s)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd     = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Carver        = FileName,
          CarverCmd     = ProcessCommandLine,
          DropTime, DroppedFile, DroppedPath,
          InitiatingProcessSHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT37 / ScarCruft — findstr against a .lnk file (SecurityEvent 4688)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\findstr.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\find.exe"
| where CommandLine has ".lnk"
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\cmd.exe"
   or ParentProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe"
   or ParentProcessName endswith "\\pwsh.exe"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcess = ParentProcessName,
          NewProcess    = NewProcessName,
          CommandLine,
          SubjectUserName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT37 ScarCruft LNK Payload Extraction via FindStr on Parent LNK
id: 4f1e8d22-c731-49a2-b8f0-apt37-lnk-findstr
status: experimental
description: |
  APT37 (ScarCruft / Reaper / Group123) weaponised LNK lures invoke findstr.exe against the parent .lnk file
  to carve out an embedded payload + decoy document. This findstr-on-LNK pattern is the canonical ROKRAT,
  M2RAT and CHINOTTO loader pattern documented across Talos, Genians, AhnLab and Kaspersky reporting.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0067/
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/rokrat-analysis
  - https://www.genians.co.kr/en/blog/threat_intelligence_report_apt37
  - https://asec.ahnlab.com/en/m2rat-scarcruft
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/11
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.t1027
  - attack.t1140
  - attack.g0067
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\findstr.exe'
      - '\find.exe'
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: '.lnk'
  condition: selection
fields:
  - Image
  - ParentImage
  - CommandLine
  - User
falsepositives:
  - IT admins / power users manually grep'ing into a shortcut file (very rare in practice)
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT37's weaponised LNK loader has used `cmd.exe /c findstr.exe "<marker>" "<full lnk path>" > <temp>` (and PowerShell equivalents reading the parent LNK) as its first-stage payload-carve mechanism since at least 2022, observed in RoKRAT, M2RAT and CHINOTTO campaigns targeting South Korean policy, defector and journalist communities (per Cisco Talos, Genians, AhnLab ASEC, ESET). The findstr-against
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
